title: Old Scent Of The Plum-Tree
content:
Remembering what passed
    Under the scent of the plum-tree,
    I asked the plum-tree for tidings
    Of that other.
    Alas ... the cold moon of spring....

    From the Japanese of Fujiwara Ietaka. (1158-1237).
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator), titled "Old Scent Of The Plum-Tree", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
